1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم ويل للمطففين
1.english: Woe to those who give less [than due],

1.arabic: الذين إذا اكتالوا على الناس يستوفون
1.english: Who, when they take a measure from people, take in full.
But if they give by measure or by weight to them, they cause loss.
1.transliteration: Wa izaa kaaloohum aw wazanoohum yukhsiroon

1.english: Do they not think that they will be resurrected
For a tremendous Day -
The Day when mankind will stand before the Lord of the worlds?

1.arabic: كلا إن كتاب الفجار لفي سجين
1.english: No! Indeed, the record of the wicked is in sijjeen.
1.transliteration: Kallaaa inna kitaabal fujjaari lafee Sijjeen

1.english: And what can make you know what is sijjeen?
It is [their destination recorded in] a register inscribed.
Woe, that Day, to the deniers,
Who deny the Day of Recompense.

1.arabic: وما يكذب به إلا كل معتد أثيم
1.english: And none deny it except every sinful transgressor.
1.transliteration: Wa maa yukazzibu biheee illaa kullu mu'tadin aseem

1.arabic: إذا تتلى عليه آياتنا قال أساطير الأولين
1.english: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, "Legends of the former peoples."
1.transliteration: Izaa tutlaa'alaihi aayaatunaa qaala asaateerul awwaleen

1.arabic: كلا ۖ بل ۜ ران على قلوبهم ما كانوا يكسبون
1.english: No! Rather, the stain has covered their hearts of that which they were earning.
1.transliteration: Kallaa bal raana 'alaa quloobihim maa kaanoo yaksiboon

1.arabic: كلا إنهم عن ربهم يومئذ لمحجوبون
1.english: No! Indeed, from their Lord, that Day, they will be partitioned.
1.transliteration: Kallaaa innahum 'ar Rabbihim yawma'izil lamah jooboon

1.english: Then indeed, they will [enter and] burn in Hellfire.

1.arabic: ثم يقال هذا الذي كنتم به تكذبون
1.english: Then it will be said [to them], "This is what you used to deny."
1.transliteration: Summa yuqaalu haazal lazee kuntum bihee tukazziboon

1.arabic: كلا إن كتاب الأبرار لفي عليين
1.english: No! Indeed, the record of the righteous is in 'illiyyun.
And what can make you know what is 'illiyyun?
It is [their destination recorded in] a register inscribed
Which is witnessed by those brought near [to Allah].
Indeed, the righteous will be in pleasure
On adorned couches, observing.
You will recognize in their faces the radiance of pleasure.
They will be given to drink [pure] wine [which was] sealed.

1.arabic: ختامه مسك ۚ وفي ذلك فليتنافس المتنافسون
1.english: The last of it is musk. So for this let the competitors compete.
1.transliteration: Khitaamuhoo misk; wa fee zaalika falyatanaafasil Mutanaafisoon

1.english: And its mixture is of Tasneem,
A spring from which those near [to Allah] drink.

1.arabic: إن الذين أجرموا كانوا من الذين آمنوا يضحكون
1.english: Indeed, those who committed crimes used to laugh at those who believed.
1.transliteration: Innal lazeena ajramoo kaanoo minal lazeena aamanoo yadhakoon

1.english: And when they passed by them, they would exchange derisive glances.

1.arabic: وإذا انقلبوا إلى أهلهم انقلبوا فكهين
1.english: And when they returned to their people, they would return jesting.
1.transliteration: Wa izan qalabooo ilaaa ahlihimun qalaboo fakiheen

1.arabic: وإذا رأوهم قالوا إن هؤلاء لضالون
1.english: And when they saw them, they would say, "Indeed, those are truly lost."
1.transliteration: Wa izaa ra awhum qaalooo inna haaa'ulaaa'i ladaaal loon

1.english: But they had not been sent as guardians over them.

1.arabic: فاليوم الذين آمنوا من الكفار يضحكون
1.english: So Today those who believed are laughing at the disbelievers,
1.transliteration: Fal yawmal lazeena aamanoo minal kuffaari yadhakoon

1.english: On adorned couches, observing.

1.arabic: هل ثوب الكفار ما كانوا يفعلون
1.english: Have the disbelievers [not] been rewarded [this Day] for what they used to do?
1.transliteration: Hal suwwibal kuffaaru maa kaanoo yaf'aloon
